_sentiment_note: Gemini 2.5 Flash is primary USD reasoning engine. GBP pairs remain pure technical. FinBERT kept as automatic fallback.

aggressive._note: v2.7.0: ADX 15->20. ADX<20=no confirmed trend=no edge for trend-following.

conservative._note: v2.7.1: threshold 57→50. Requires strong directional bias but reachable under normal trending conditions.

tiers._note: Tier A (72-79) BLOCKED — 0% WR historically. Tier C (<55) BLOCKED. A+ (>=80) and B (55-71) trade.

sessions._note: v2.4.0: EU session delayed to 09:30 UTC — 08:00-09:30 is noise zone. v2.5.0: session end guard added (see exit_rules).

exit_rules._note: v2.7.0 exits: TP1(2.5xATR) | SL(2.0xATR) | EMA20 structural invalidation (BUY closes if 1H close < EMA20) | 4h stall (neg PnL after 4h = dead trade) | 48h TTL (garbage collection for stateless cron) | Friday 21:00 UTC kill (no weekend gap exposure).

mtf_settings._note: v2.5.0: 30m and 1h REMOVED from MTF — signal is now generated on 1h chart. MTF only checks above signal timeframe: 4h (required), Daily (decisive), Weekly (bonus only, never blocks). Daily would have blocked the entire March 23 -318 pip day.

gemini_usd_gate._note: gemini-2.5-flash only. NOT 2.0-flash (deprecated Feb 2026, retired Mar 3 2026). Free tier: 10 RPM, 250 RPD. NewsAPI headlines passed as text context — no Google Search grounding ($35/1K). BULLISH=allow BUY USD, BEARISH=allow SELL USD, NEUTRAL=block all USD. Falls back to FinBERT if GEMINI_API_KEY not set.

advanced._cache_note: v2.5.0: 60min cache TTL appropriate for 1h signal timeframe (was 5min for 15m).

session_thresholds._note: v2.6.0-FOCUSED: US session ONLY. European blocked (999) — 75 trades 22.7% WR -599 pips. LATE_US blocked — thin volume, not edge target. Only US active: 48t 37.5% WR +79 pips.

pair_limits._note: v2.7.1: USDJPY re-enabled (limit=3, US session only, Gemini gate applies). GBPUSD+GBPJPY+USDJPY. All others remain 0.

GBPUSD.reason: GBPUSD SELL: 0/3 wins historically (-49.7p). BUY: 39%+ WR. BoE structural bid 2025-26.

directional_filters._note: Block specific directions for pairs with confirmed directional bias.

validation._age_note: v2.7.0: signal age checked via TTL (48h), not max_signal_age_seconds.

session_expiry_minutes._note: v2.7.0: 120->480 min. Structural exits (EMA20, 4h stall) need room to work. 48h TTL is the hard ceiling.

min_sl_pips._note: v2.5.0: reduced slightly — 1h ATR is larger so absolute pip SL is still adequate at 2.0x.

usd_pair_rules._note: v2.7.1: min_score 60→55 (USDJPY re-enabled; 1H scoring with new threshold=50 makes 55 the appropriate USD floor).

sentiment._setup: HF_API_KEY = FinBERT fallback. GEMINI_API_KEY = primary USD gate. Both secrets needed for full functionality.

risk_management._note: v2.5.0: 1h signals with 2h time stop means positions close faster — 5 positions is reasonable.

performance_tuning._target_note: v2.5.0: target WR 0.65→0.42. With 1.25 R:R (TP1/SL), breakeven is ~44%. Realistic target 38-45%.

economic_calendar._note: High-impact events only. With 2h time stop, single news event impact is contained — but blackout still prevents entering INTO the event window.

_research_notes.adx_threshold: ADX<20 = market not trending. Trend signals have no edge in ADX 15-20 zone. All three AIs (ChatGPT, Kimi, Gemini) independently agreed on raising to 20.
_research_notes.atr_pct_filter: Kimi derived from April 2026 GBPUSD 1H data. ATR%_ratio < 0.85 means stops are too tight relative to normal noise. April 14: ADX=40 but ratio=0.69-0.76, effective SL ~13 pips = easily hunted. Filter blocks without requiring any API.
_research_notes.ema20_structural_exit: Gemini identified that 120-min time stop was killing trades before 2.5xATR could develop. EMA20 cross is market-structure-based — closes BUY when 1H bar closes below EMA20 (trend broken). Allows trades the breathing room a 1H signal needs.
_research_notes.stall_exit: Replace 45-min/10-pip momentum check (too tight for 1H) with 4h stall: if open 4h+ AND PnL negative, trade is dead. Gives 1H setups room through normal pullback/retest phases.
_research_notes.friday_kill: yfinance cannot model weekend gap slippage. Hard close at Friday 21:00 UTC prevents silently distorted performance data.
_research_notes.session_focus: US session 37.5% WR +79 pips vs European 22.7% WR -599 pips on same pairs. v2.6.0-FOCUSED: US session only, GBPUSD+GBPJPY only.
_research_notes.breakeven_WR: TP1=2.5xATR, SL=2.0xATR → R:R=1.25 → breakeven=44.4%. GBPUSD at 44% WR historically is at breakeven. Structural exits and ATR% filter aim to move this above 44%.

_v2.7.0_changes: SMART EXIT (Claude+ChatGPT+Kimi+Gemini research). 1) ADX 15->20 (all AIs agreed; ADX<20=not trending). 2) ATR% regime filter: threshold=0.85, lookback=100 bars, per-pair. 3) 120-min time stop REMOVED -> EMA20 structural invalidation. 4) 45-min momentum check REMOVED -> 4h stall exit (neg PnL after 4h). 5) 48h TTL garbage collection. 6) Friday 21:00 UTC kill switch.

atr_pct_filter._note: v2.7.1: threshold lowered 0.85→0.75 (May 2026 lower volatility regime). Was blocking ~50-60% of bars instead of intended 30%.
